midjourneyPlanContext: manual generation; no automation; generated images not committed
prompt: Generic operational dashboard interface for local commerce inventory review, focused on fast scanability. Show grouped metrics, a compact table, readable hierarchy, clear status chips, and large enough controls. No logos, no brand names, no real product references, no private data, no recognizable people.
negativePromptNotes: Avoid brand imitation, private data, tiny controls, unreadable density, and product-specific layout copying.
referenceIntent: Create visual variety for dense-dashboard scanability calibration without copying the generated reference.

rightsReview.notes: Prompt avoids brands, private data, and living artist style. No generated asset is committed.

excludedUse: model training on generated assets without separate approval

midjourneyPlanContext: manual generation; no automation; generated images not committed
prompt: Generic operational dashboard interface for local commerce inventory review, intentionally hard to scan. Show overcrowded cards, weak grouping, tiny controls, long table labels, and unclear priority. No logos, no brand names, no real product references, no private data, no recognizable people.
negativePromptNotes: Avoid brand imitation, private data, and copying any generated image composition exactly.
referenceIntent: Create a generic hand-authored stress fixture for scanability and responsive-density risks.

rightsReview.notes: Prompt avoids brands, private data, and living artist style. No generated asset is committed.

expectedFindings.summary: Fixed-width dense board is expected to create mobile horizontal overflow.
expectedFindings.notes: Current viewport sweep can observe document overflow.

expectedFindings.summary: Tiny icon-only actions are expected to create target-size risk.
expectedFindings.notes: Current computed-style measurement can observe small target geometry.

expectedFindings.summary: Long table notes are expected to create a readability line-length risk.
expectedFindings.notes: This is a heuristic scanability signal, not an objective failure.

expectedFindings.summary: Overall information hierarchy is intentionally overloaded and should remain a human review prompt until a future density criterion exists.
expectedFindings.notes: Reference-derived subjective density should not become deterministic without a source-backed criterion.

excludedUse: model training on generated assets without separate approval

midjourneyPlanContext: manual generation by user; no automation; generated image stored only under ignored local-assets path
prompt: Generic operational dashboard interface for local commerce inventory review, intentionally hard to scan. Show overcrowded cards, weak grouping, tiny controls, long table labels, and unclear priority. No logos, no brand names, no real product references, no private data, no recognizable people.
negativePromptNotes: Avoid brand imitation, private data, and copying any generated image composition exactly.
referenceDescription: Bad dense dashboard reference with unified color tone but unclear priority levels. It is difficult to tell which panels matter most, and the bottom table has weak row and column legibility.
referenceIntent: Calibrate negative dense-dashboard examples where visual consistency alone does not create usable hierarchy or table scanability.

rightsReview.notes: User-selected bad reference. Generated image remains local-only and is not committed.

expectedFindings.summary: Overall priority levels are unclear even though the color palette is visually consistent.
expectedFindings.notes: This is a reference-derived hierarchy judgment and should not become deterministic until a source-backed criterion exists.

expectedFindings.summary: The bottom table is visually difficult to scan because rows and columns do not create clear reading paths.
expectedFindings.notes: Current checks can catch some overflow/readability issues, but table-specific scanability needs a future criterion.

excludedUse: model training on generated assets without separate approval

midjourneyPlanContext: manual generation by user; no automation; generated image stored only under ignored local-assets path
prompt: Generic operational dashboard interface for local commerce inventory review, intentionally hard to scan. Show overcrowded cards, weak grouping, tiny controls, long table labels, and unclear priority. No logos, no brand names, no real product references, no private data, no recognizable people.
negativePromptNotes: Avoid brand imitation, private data, and copying any generated image composition exactly.
referenceDescription: Bad dense dashboard reference where many modules share similar visual weight. Color hierarchy does not make important and secondary information easy to distinguish.
referenceIntent: Calibrate negative dense-dashboard examples where color and tonal hierarchy fail to explain priority across a high-density layout.

rightsReview.notes: User-selected bad reference. Generated image remains local-only and is not committed.

expectedFindings.summary: Color hierarchy does not clearly separate important information from secondary information.
expectedFindings.notes: The issue is visible to a reviewer but not yet backed by an implemented deterministic measurement.

expectedFindings.summary: High-density modules compete for attention because the layout does not establish strong priority levels.
expectedFindings.notes: Keep this as a future density and priority criterion rather than a deterministic failure for now.

midjourneyPlanContext: manual generation by user; no automation; generated image stored only under ignored local-assets path
prompt: Generic operational dashboard interface for local commerce inventory review, intentionally over-colorized. Show saturated charts, competing table highlights, colorful status blocks, and unclear priority. No logos, no brand names, no real product references, no private data, no recognizable people.
negativePromptNotes: Avoid brand imitation, private data, and copying any generated image composition exactly.
referenceDescription: Bad dense dashboard reference where overly vivid red, yellow, green, and blue accents compete across charts and tables. Color draws attention everywhere, lowering overall quality and weakening priority cues.
referenceIntent: Calibrate negative dense-dashboard examples where excessive colorfulness reduces scanability even when the layout is otherwise structured.

rightsReview.notes: User-selected bad reference. Generated image remains local-only and is not committed.

expectedFindings.summary: Highly saturated accents appear in too many regions, so color stops working as a priority signal.
expectedFindings.notes: This is a reference-derived color hierarchy judgment and should stay human-reviewed until a source-backed criterion exists.

expectedFindings.summary: Alert-like colors are used as broad visual decoration, making important and secondary information harder to separate.
expectedFindings.notes: The current harness can describe the risk, but deterministic color-priority scoring is future work.

excludedUse: model training on generated assets without separate approval
